B.note: Use DOB to choose older/younger; fallback to generic సోదరుడు.

Z.note: Use DOB to choose older/younger; fallback to generic సోదరి.

FM.note: Commonly 'నానమ్మ' for father's mother. Variants exist (బామ్మా/మామ్మ).

FB.note: Use DOB between father's brother and father to choose older/younger.

FBW.note: Often addressed like an aunt; varies by family.

HB.note: Common: husband's younger brother = బావమరిది; elder brother often addressed as బావ.

BW.note: Use DOB between brother and you to select; generic fallback వదిన.

WBW.note: Formal term often 'వదిన'; many families address as అక్క/చెల్లి based on age.

BS.note: In many Telugu families, brother's children are treated as your own; exact term varies.

BD.note: In many Telugu families, brother's children are treated as your own; exact term varies.

C.note: Telugu has many precise cousin terms depending on side and age; use rules + DOB if available.
